meetings.title: Discussion with Bas
meetings.summary: Discussed Hormuz implications for MGen (Philippine power utility) with Bas ahead of client meeting. MGen operates 1.3GW coal fleet with 97% Indonesian sourcing (~$170M annual exposure), planning additional 1.2GW coal plant. Key insights: coal competitiveness improved by Hormuz but Indonesian export restrictions driving price floor up; 40% of coal contracts expire end-2026, new contracts likely at higher prices; renewables getting "new shine" but grid resilience challenges remain. Bas to share Wood Mackenzie Philippines report, LNG supply-demand data, and Vietnam LNG-to-power frameworks. Jamie to send latest Hormuz materials to Bas for comparison.

meetings.title: Catch-up with Gwen
meetings.summary: Gwen is considering extending her LOA through June/July and evaluating whether to take the Young Whiskey marketing role or return to consulting with flex arrangements. She needs lighter workload with WFH flexibility and flexible hours due to transport time between QC and BGC. Jamie advised that the marketing role requirements aren't as specialist as she fears and stressed the need for firm, rules-based flex arrangements rather than fluid week-to-week decisions. Gwen will reassess her family situation after returning from her trip tonight and define hard rules for work arrangements before making a final decision.
